$comment: Pinned, SHA-verified FreeType SOURCE tarball for the M7 runtime-UI text substrate (src/packages/ui/text/, issue #237 / R-UI-005). SINGLE source of truth for the pin — read by cmake/ContextFreetype.cmake (string(JSON)) which fetches + verifies (cmake/ContextDownload.cmake) + extracts + add_subdirectory's FreeType's own first-class CMake to build the `freetype` target. Unlike the MSVC/Clang-ABI prebuilts (V8/wgpu-native/CEF/wasmtime — fetched as opaque binaries), FreeType is portable C built FROM SOURCE here (the WAMR-from-pinned-source precedent), so it compiles under the local Strawberry-GCC dev gate AND all three CI build legs with no prebuilt. LICENSE ELECTION: FreeType is dual-licensed 'FTL OR GPL-2.0-or-later' (docs/FTL.TXT / docs/GPLv2.TXT) — we ELECT the FreeType License (FTL), NEVER the GPL limb (DECISION-a7a8-deps.md, owner-delegated Fable verdict = GO). The build DISABLES every optional subsystem (FT_DISABLE_ZLIB/BZIP2/PNG/HARFBUZZ/BROTLI): embedded raw TTFs need none, and disabling HarfBuzz severs the FreeType<->HarfBuzz circular option + minimizes the attack surface (R-SEC-006). The official release tarball (make-dist, byte-stable — NOT a regenerated git-archive) is fetched from the FreeType home on savannah; the pin below is that artifact's SHA-256. Upstream LICENSE.TXT/FTL.TXT/GPLv2.TXT are vendored verbatim under third_party/freetype/ (NOTICE flow-down); tools/license-allowlist.json records 'freetype':'FTL'.
